mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with increasing residential mortgage debt indicate a synchronized expansion in consumer credit and construction activity, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which directly improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal reverses direction during supply-side shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power after the Federal Reserve explicitly anchors inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive information coefficients in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth is included as a control variable.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding economic activity, which increases corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the signal shows no statistical significance after adjusting for seasonal factors.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 700 thousand units while industrial production growth accelerates, it signals a broad-based demand surge that lifts cyclical equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint threshold fails to predict positive returns in out-of-sample validation, or the signal reverses to negative when real interest rates exceed 2.0 percent.
